_description: The Affordance Ontology (Article XXVIII). Every entity — item, NPC, beast, plant, technique, formation, location, structure — exposes AFFORDANCES: verbs describing what can meaningfully occur with it. The UI discovers these. The AI discovers these. Nothing special-cases any entity. Two entities are mechanically unique when they expose different verbs. This is DATA, not a Java class (Article XXVI).

_design_notes.discovery_not_hardcoding: Systems must not hardcode 'if entity == spirit_herb then harvest().' Instead, they read the entity's affordances array and present/enable those verbs. A new entity with a new affordance requires no code change — only a data change.
_design_notes.verb_naming: Affordances are single verbs or short verb phrases, lowercase, snake_case. Examples: harvest, transplant, smell, refine, study, steal, trade, bait, ask, observe, debate, learn, challenge, spar, request_advice, travel_together.
_design_notes.conditional_affordances: An affordance may have conditions — requirements that must be met before the verb is available. A spirit herb cannot be 'refined' without an alchemy furnace nearby; Wang Lin cannot be 'requested_technique' without demonstrated judgment.
_design_notes.affordance_vs_desire: Affordances are what CAN be done WITH an entity (player- or NPC-facing verbs). Desires (desire_state_schema) are what an NPC WANTS to do TO others. An NPC's affordances describe how others can engage it; its desires describe how it engages others. Both are needed.
_design_notes.perception_coupling: An affordance may be hidden behind a perception tier (Article VI: reality -> perception). A mortal sees a spirit herb and affords only 'smell, pick.' A Qi Condensation cultivator sees the same herb and affords 'harvest, transplant, refine, study.' The herb has not changed; perception has.

schema.entity_id: string — the entity's id (npc_id, item_id, block_id, species_id, structure_id, location_id)
schema.entity_type: enum — npc | item | block | beast | plant | technique | formation | structure | location | artifact

affordances.label: string — player-facing label, e.g. 'Harvest', 'Ask about herbs'
affordances.description: string — what happens when this affordance is invoked
affordances.perception_required: enum|null — mortal | qi_condensation | foundation_establishment | core_formation | nascent_soul | soul_formation | soul_transformation | ancient_god | null (null = always visible)
affordances.initiates_engine: enum|null — if invoking this affordance triggers a social engine, which one. e.g. asking Wang Lin for a technique initiates 'request'. null for non-social affordances like 'smell'.
affordances.cost: object|null — what the invocation costs (items, time, favor, reputation). null if free.
affordances.outcome: string — the canonical outcome. May be probabilistic — see outcome_variants.
outcome_variants: string — alternative outcomes based on conditions/state. The engine selects among these.
affordances.memory_tag: string|null — tag written to memory ledger when this affordance is invoked (for significant interactions)

spirit_herb.note: From Article XXVIII. A mortal sees only 'smell, pick.' A cultivator sees all eight.

li_muwan.note: From Article XXVIII. Li Muwan's affordances reflect her role as an alchemist, not a generic quest giver.

wang_lin.note: From Article XXVIII. Wang Lin's affordances include the protagonist-copy mechanic (request_artifact_copy, request_technique) which he evaluates via judgment, not transaction — see wang_lin_bidirectional_protocol.json.
